categories.label: Established

repos.name: awesome
repos.description: 😎 Awesome lists about all kinds of interesting topics
repos.summary_short: A curated index of awesome lists covering software, technology, and many other topics.
repos.summary_medium: This repository is a central directory of community-curated "awesome" lists spanning programming, tools, resources, and a wide range of interests. It helps people discover high-quality collections of links organized by topic rather than serving as a single software project. Its long-running popularity and broad scope make it a common starting point for exploring new domains.
repos.trending_reason: It is likely gaining attention because it remains one of GitHub’s most recognized discovery hubs and continues to receive fresh updates, which keeps it visible to users looking for curated learning and tooling resources. Its very high star count combined with a top Rising Stars rank this week suggests renewed community traffic around repository discovery and reference collections.

repos.name: freeCodeCamp

repos.summary_short: freeCodeCamp is an open-source platform and curriculum for learning coding, math, and computer science for free.
repos.summary_medium: This repository contains the codebase and curriculum for freeCodeCamp.org, a free learning platform focused on programming, math, and computer science. It supports an educational experience around JavaScript and related web technologies, with topics spanning certifications, community, and teaching resources. As an established nonprofit project, it combines open-source software with structured learning content.
repos.trending_reason: The repository is likely drawing renewed attention because it remains actively maintained while sitting near the top of the Rising Stars ranking this week. Its massive community, recognizable educational mission, and open curriculum make updates especially visible across developers, learners, and educators.

repos.name: linux
repos.description: Linux kernel source tree
repos.summary_short: The Linux kernel source tree contains the core operating system kernel used by Linux-based systems.
repos.summary_medium: This repository hosts the Linux kernel, the core low-level software that manages hardware, memory, processes, and system interfaces in Linux-based operating systems. Written primarily in C, it is one of the most widely used and actively developed open source codebases. It serves as the foundation for servers, desktops, embedded devices, and many other computing environments.
repos.trending_reason: It is likely drawing renewed attention because it remains one of the most active and foundational infrastructure repositories, with very recent pushes signaling ongoing development. Its placement in the Rising Stars category suggests sustained community engagement and continued relevance across the broader systems and infrastructure ecosystem.
